mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs per month signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a strong economic expansion with stable pricing, creating favorable conditions for positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases > 2% YoY and CPIAUCSL decreases > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 60%+ of triggered periods, or the joint effect of GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with improved corporate margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 55%+ of INDPRO growth periods, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts (HOUST) indicate stronger demand for residential construction, which drives related sectors and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of HOUST growth periods, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) combined with elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of triggered periods, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong household financial health and labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases > 1% MoM and UNRATE decreases > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 55%+ of triggered periods, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.02 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in 2-year rolling windows.
